notes.anchor: all the tribunes of the plebs
notes.note: The ten tribunes of the plebs, magistrates with the power to propose legislation and to veto. Cicero's recall from exile would have to be carried by a tribunician bill, so a friendly board of tribunes was essential to his hopes.
P. Cornelius Lentulus Spinther, consul-designate for 57 BC, who became the chief champion of Cicero's recall and carried it through the next year.

notes.anchor: Plancius, the most attentive of men
notes.note: Cn. Plancius, quaestor of Macedonia in 58 BC, who sheltered the exiled Cicero at Thessalonica and Dyrrhachium. Cicero later defended him in the Pro Plancio (54 BC).
The Kalends were the first day of the Roman month; the Romans counted backward inclusively from it, so the date here is 26 November 58 BC.
